text: Dude, I'm such a mongrel. I'm from India originally, but have moved just a bunch of countries from the age of 12, so a little bit from everywhere.
context: V describing his international background and identity when asked about his origins.

text: It is always nice to sort of take that time aside and increasingly, the folks that I find that are like around and that I notice the most are also generally the ones that I enjoy getting to know the most.
context: V reflecting on how professional networking intuition develops over time.

text: You develop that intuition for who you're going to enjoy, like, spending time with after enough of these.
context: V on the pattern recognition that comes from extensive networking experience.

text: There's a lot to career coaching that leads them to making terrible fucking decisions using auto apply tools - some real dog shit tools out there that are just sucking people's money up.
context: V critiquing low-quality competitors in the job search tool space while explaining Careerspan's pivot.

text: Holy shit, we've built this like really high signal community of folks. And unlike every other platform, we have deep data on them.
context: V articulating Careerspan's competitive advantage - the combination of community and depth of data.

text: We like to think of skills as like clusters of co-occurring things - comorbidities is the wrong word, but you know, like the co-occurring things.
context: V explaining Careerspan's skills model as interconnected clusters rather than isolated capabilities.

text: From their reflections and from their experiences - what are the strengths for this job and what are the weaknesses that we would infer based on our understanding of what is the necessary standard for this job.
context: V explaining how Careerspan provides candidate insights based on narrative data rather than resume keywords.
